summary: Package self-identifies as malware delivery scaffold with missing payload module
details: @jaymara/jsononifier@1.0.1 is published under a cover story of 'Lightweight JSON formatting and validation utilities' but its index.js contains the literal source comment 'Import trigger to enable payload (looks like normal initialization)' next to require('./lib/trigger'). The package.json declares main 'index.js' and ships 'lib/' in the files field; index.js requires./lib/formatter,./lib/validator, and./lib/trigger, but none of these modules are present in the tarball. As shipped, require('@jaymara/jsononifier') throws because lib/trigger is missing, so the load-time payload does not execute in this specific version. However, the in-source self-description of a hidden payload disguised as initialization, combined with the cover-story package description and the staged-but-incomplete file layout, identifies this release as a staging artifact for a malware delivery package rather than a legitimate utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
